title: Upon Parrat.
content:
Parrat protests 'tis he, and only he
    Can teach a man the art of memory:
    Believe him not; for he forgot it quite,
    Being drunk, who 'twas that can'd his ribs last night.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Upon Parrat."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
